title: aloe viscosa var. indurata
intro: Aloe viscosa var. indurata, also known as the "hairy aloe" or "gum plant", is a succulent plant species within the Aloe genus. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as aloin and aloe-emodin. The health benefits of Aloe viscosa var. indurata include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and aiding in wound healing, making it a potential natural remedy for various skin conditions and injuries. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting its gel and latex, which can be used to create topical creams, ointments, and gels for treating skin issues.
